meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: How extensive is Russia’s military aid to Iran?
items.summary: Russia has long exchanged weapons with Iran and is likely offering satellite support, but lacks the will to do much more

items.title: Now in power, Nepal’s rapper-politician Balen Shah faces new challenge
items.summary: Shah was a rebel — crude and different from the politicians who previously led Nepal. How will he be in office?

items.title: How can students in Gaza continue to learn with no universities?
items.summary: We look at the hardships of students in Gaza trying to pursue higher education after Israel destroyed all universities.

items.title: Israeli opposition leader rails against ‘multi-front war without strategy’
items.summary: Yair Lapid says military 'is stretched to the limit and beyond' as Israeli forces wage attacks on Iran and Lebanon.

items.title: Israel’s opposition says military forces are ‘in collapse’
items.summary: Yair Lapid has criticises the government and says military forces are ‘in collapse.'

items.title: Iran bans sports teams from travelling to ‘hostile’ countries amid war
items.summary: Sports Ministry says ban for 'security' of athletes before a game between Iranian and Emirati clubs in Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Italy to play Bosnia in 2026 World Cup playoff final, Kosovo face Turkiye
items.summary: Elsewhere, Bolivia beat Suriname and Jamaica defeat New Caledonia in intercontinental playoff semifinals.

items.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 28 of attacks?
items.summary: Trump has delayed attacks on Iran's energy facilities by 10 days, claiming talks are going well - though Iran disagrees.

items.title: ‘Truly junk’: E-waste from rich nations floods local markets in Nigeria
items.summary: Discarded, near-end-of-life electronics shipped from developed countries are adding to Nigeria's growing e-waste burden.

items.title: The Strait of Hormuz is not just an oil chokepoint
items.summary: It is the single point of failure of globalised production.

items.title: Sri Lanka braces for new economic crisis as war on Iran continues
items.summary: For many Sri Lankans, the fuel shortage triggered by the war carries echoes of the 2022 economic crisis - with a twist.

items.title: Malaysia’s ships allowed to pass Strait of Hormuz, PM Anwar says
items.summary: Malaysian leader says oil tankers granted clearance by Iran as government introduces measures to conserve fuel.

items.title: First responders rescue man from Tehran building hit by air strike
items.summary: The Iranian Red Crescent Society released video showing rescuers in Tehran pulling a man from a damaged building.

items.title: Palestinians flee tear gas as Israeli settlers arrive in occupied West Bank
items.summary: The Israeli army fired tear gas at Palestinian residents of the Beit Imrin village in the occupied West Bank.

items.title: Red Crescent video shows aftermath of strike on homes in Iran’s Urmia
items.summary: Iranian Red Crescent video shows the aftermath of a strike on a residential area in Urmia.

items.title: Trump’s signature to appear on US currency in first for sitting president
items.summary: Announcement is the latest example of Trump literally stamping his name on US institutions.

items.title: Albanese says Australia playing “constructive” role in the war on Iran
items.summary: Australia’s prime minister says his government has made a “constructive” contribution to the war on Iran.

items.title: Endangered monarch butterfly population surges 64 percent
items.summary: Mexico's monarch butterfly population surged 64 percent this winter.

items.title: Mexico launches search for two missing aid boats bound for Cuba
items.summary: Mexico’s navy has launched a search operation after two boats carrying humanitarian aid to Cuba went missing.

items.title: Australia’s post-Bondi crackdown accused of targeting pro-Palestinian voices
items.summary: Fear for future of peaceful protest in Australia while some say Jewish community being prioritised over others.

items.title: Who is fighting in Myanmar’s multi-front civil war?
items.summary: Alliances are fluid in Myanmar's civil war, involving a military regime, ethnic armed groups and pro-democracy forces.

items.title: Analysts say Trump doesn’t understand Iran reality has changed
items.summary: Watch this discussion between Ross Harrison and Hassan Ahmadian on US strategy regarding Iran.

items.title: Former Venezuelan president Maduro seeks to throw out US drug charges
items.summary: Nicolas Maduro appeared in a New York court seeking to dismiss drug trafficking charges.

items.title: Projectile from Lebanon kills at least one in north Israel
items.summary: Video captured flames and black smoke where a projectile from Lebanon killed at least one person in northern Israel.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump delays attacks on Iranian energy sector by 10 days
items.summary: Iranian missiles, drones target Kuwait, UAE, Saudi Arabia and Jordan as Trump says Iran has 'chance to make a deal'.
